caveats: LOO clusters defined by MiniLM embeddings -- circularly favors MiniLM
Qualitative review found TF-IDF underrated by MRR -- held-out recovery is a better quality signal for TF-IDF
One month of data; seasonal variation not captured
AI qualitative review, not human domain expert
SPECTER2 score compression (0.009 spread) makes its MRR unreliable as a ranking signal

leave_one_out_mrr.interpretation: Highest MRR of any strategy. Partially inflated by circular evaluation (LOO clusters defined by MiniLM). True quality advantage is real but smaller than 4x gap over TF-IDF suggests.

seed_proximity.interpretation: Strong -- recommendations are semantically close to seeds.

topical_coherence.interpretation: Good internal coherence within recommendation sets.

cluster_diversity.interpretation: Low diversity -- recommendations cluster tightly around seed topics. This is precision, not a defect.

novelty.interpretation: Very low -- most recommendations are near seed papers. Good for focused retrieval, bad for discovery.

category_surprise.interpretation: Rarely surprises with out-of-category papers. Stays within expected categories.

coverage.interpretation: Recovers 69% of held-out cluster members in top-20. Best coverage of any strategy.

character.feels_like: A well-read colleague who knows the semantic neighborhood but sometimes conflates related-sounding work
character.best_for: Papers semantically similar to seeds, especially for narrow/distinctive topics
character.worst_for: Papers that describe the same concept with different vocabulary; papers from adjacent paradigms that should not be included but share terms
character.failure_mode: Adjacent-paradigm conflation (e.g., imitation learning in an RL search)
character.score_interpretation: Moderate spread; ranking carries some information but adjacent ranks are noisy

resources.note: One-time embedding cost for 19K papers. GPU recommended but not required.

leave_one_out_mrr.interpretation: Low MRR, but this is partly misleading. Score compression (0.009 spread) means ranking is essentially random within SPECTER2's top results. MRR penalizes this heavily.

seed_proximity.interpretation: Moderate -- closer to seeds than random but less focused than MiniLM.

topical_coherence.interpretation: Lower coherence reflects broader, more cross-community results.

cluster_diversity.interpretation: Similar to MiniLM in cluster count.

novelty.interpretation: Slightly more novel than MiniLM -- surfaces some papers MiniLM does not.

category_surprise.interpretation: More category surprises than MiniLM -- reflects cross-community discovery character.

coverage.interpretation: Half of MiniLM's coverage. Cannot reliably recover held-out papers.

character.feels_like: A department librarian who knows who cites whom but cannot rank within a community
character.best_for: Cross-community papers, field infrastructure (benchmarks, production tools), well-connected work from major labs
character.worst_for: Papers outside the citation community; fine-grained relevance distinctions
character.failure_mode: Occasional wild misfires from citation-graph noise (e.g., classical computing library in quantum ML results); score compression prevents meaningful ranking
character.score_interpretation: Near-zero spread -- ranking is effectively meaningless noise. Cannot distinguish #1 from #1000.

resources.note: 14x slower to embed than MiniLM. GPU strongly recommended. int8 quantization NOT recommended (fails quality threshold for SPECTER2).

S1c.standalone_reason: Score compression prevents standalone ranking. Must be used as a secondary view or diversity signal, not as a primary retrieval strategy.

leave_one_out_mrr.interpretation: Lowest MRR of viable strategies, BUT this is the most egregious evaluation bias. TF-IDF outperforms both embedding strategies on held-out recovery (5/15 vs 2/15 for MiniLM, 0/15 for SPECTER2). MRR systematically underrates TF-IDF because LOO clusters are embedding-defined.

seed_proximity.interpretation: Lower than embeddings -- expected since TF-IDF captures keyword overlap, not semantic proximity.

topical_coherence.interpretation: Moderate -- keyword-matched sets are less topically tight than embedding-matched sets.

cluster_diversity.interpretation: More diverse than embeddings -- keyword matching crosses cluster boundaries.

novelty.interpretation: Low but higher than MiniLM -- finds some unexpected papers via keyword routes.

category_surprise.interpretation: More cross-category than either embedding strategy.

coverage.interpretation: Low cluster coverage, but this metric is circular (embedding-defined clusters). Held-out recovery tells a different story: 5/15 vs 2/15.

character.feels_like: A keyword search with intelligent ranking -- literal but precise
character.best_for: Papers using exact same terminology as seeds; held-out recovery; medium-breadth topics where interest overlaps with adjacent paradigms
character.worst_for: Papers that use different terminology for the same concepts; papers whose relevance is conceptual rather than terminological
character.failure_mode: Papers that mention keywords without being primarily about the topic (false positives from vocabulary overlap)
character.score_interpretation: Widest spread of any strategy -- ranking carries genuine information. Clear separation between relevant and irrelevant.

resources.note: TF-IDF matrix rebuilt on startup, not persisted. Latency is per-query at 19K; drops with pre-filtering.

context_sensitivity.scale: DEGRADES at scale. -54% MRR at synthetic 50K. Vocabulary growth dilutes term discrimination.
context_sensitivity.breadth: DEGRADES with breadth. -27% relative to narrow. Broad interests span too much vocabulary.
context_sensitivity.cold_start: Peaks at 5-10 seeds. Below 3 seeds, vocabulary is too sparse for meaningful TF-IDF matching.

S1g.elimination_reason: 74% MRR collapse (0.104 vs 0.398 for title+abstract). Title-only embeddings lose critical abstract information. Not viable as a standalone strategy.

leave_one_out_mrr.interpretation: Same as TF-IDF -- title-only MiniLM has lost its semantic advantage and degrades to keyword-level quality.

title_vs_abstract_cosine.interpretation: Only 73% similarity between title and title+abstract embeddings. Title captures different information than abstract.

S1h.elimination_reason: 77% MRR collapse (0.042 vs 0.184). SPECTER2 title-only is worse than random for most profiles.

leave_one_out_mrr.interpretation: Near-zero. Title-only SPECTER2 adapter is not functional.

title_vs_abstract_cosine.interpretation: 95% similarity suggests SPECTER2 title embeddings are very close to full embeddings, yet MRR collapses. The small differences are load-bearing.

S1i.elimination_reason: Same quality as TF-IDF cosine (MRR 0.099 vs 0.104) at approximately 10x latency (~2s training per query). No quality benefit justifies the computational overhead.

leave_one_out_mrr.interpretation: Statistically indistinguishable from TF-IDF cosine. The SVM does not learn a better boundary than simple cosine similarity.

S1j.elimination_reason: Mathematical identity with S1a. Normalized embeddings + cosine similarity = unnormalized centroid + cosine similarity for unit-norm vectors. MRR identical to 6 decimal places.

leave_one_out_mrr.interpretation: Identical to S1a. This confirms the mathematical expectation and eliminates a configuration knob.
Near-zero MRR. Categories are too coarse for ranking -- cs.AI has 5438 papers. Useful as pre-filter, not as ranker.

seed_proximity.interpretation: Above random (0.282) -- category papers are somewhat related to seeds.

coverage.interpretation: Minimal coverage -- most cluster members span multiple categories.

S2a.role: Pre-filter to reduce candidate set before applying content-based strategies. Seed proximity 0.454 confirms it selects relevant subsets.

leave_one_out_mrr.interpretation: Near-zero. Author overlap is too sparse for recommendation.

coverage.interpretation: 0.2% coverage -- almost no papers are reachable through co-authorship in a single month.

S2f.role: Tiny signal. Could provide a 'from same lab' badge but not a recommendation strategy.

S2d.elimination_reason: Only 2.6% enrichment coverage. FWCI values exist for too few papers to be useful as a signal. Requires full OpenAlex enrichment expansion.
S2d.note: FWCI has strong theoretical properties (r=0.75 with citations, field-normalized) but needs enrichment for 50%+ of corpus to be a viable signal.

S2e.elimination_reason: Same enrichment limitation as S2d. Also, January 2026 papers have near-zero citations -- too recent for citation signals.
S2e.note: Citation-based signals are fundamentally limited for recent papers (< 6 months old).

leave_one_out_mrr.interpretation: Zero MRR because only 1 of 120 seed papers has referenced_works data. The strategy literally cannot score seed papers.

seed_proximity.interpretation: Slightly above random (0.282). The 95 papers with reference data show some topical structure.

focused_evaluation.interpretation: When reference data is available, bibliographic coupling DOES discriminate topically related papers (discrimination 0.467 > 0). The algorithm works; coverage must expand from 95/19252 to be useful.

S3c.elimination_reason: Zero papers have related_works populated in OpenAlex cache. Field appears to not be returned for this corpus.

leave_one_out_mrr.interpretation: Floor. Any working strategy must beat this.

seed_proximity.interpretation: Random proximity level. Calibration point for all other strategies.

topical_coherence.interpretation: Random coherence. Base rate for the corpus.

coverage.interpretation: Zero coverage -- random never hits cluster members in top-20.

leave_one_out_mrr.interpretation: Barely above random. Category alone is nearly useless for ranking.

seed_proximity.interpretation: Better than random but below all content strategies. Category provides a rough filter.

combinations.finding: Fusion degrades MiniLM. Pipeline converges to MiniLM. Strategies should be parallel views, not merged.

unique_held_out_recoveries.note: 9 unique papers recovered across all strategies, with only partial overlap. Strategies ARE complementary in what they find.

best_rrf.interpretation: RRF hurts MiniLM because TF-IDF's different ranking permutes MiniLM's good order. The low-MRR strategy drags down the high-MRR strategy.

best_weighted.interpretation: Weighting toward MiniLM helps but still degrades. Even 70/30 weighting cannot avoid the penalty.

best_pipeline.vs_s1a_alone: Bit-identical. Category pre-filter selects the same papers MiniLM would rank highly.
best_pipeline.interpretation: All pipeline architectures converge to MiniLM standalone. The pre-filter does not help because MiniLM already ranks category-relevant papers at the top.

why_fusion_fails: Strategies value different quality dimensions (semantic precision vs keyword precision vs community connectivity)
These dimensions are INCOMMENSURABLE -- there is no single number that correctly weighs 'semantically similar' against 'uses the same keywords'
RRF/weighted fusion forces a single ranking, which necessarily compromises one dimension for another
The complementarity is real (Jaccard 0.179, 9 unique recoveries) but must be surfaced as PARALLEL VIEWS, not collapsed into one ranking

top_k.recommendation: K=20 default (good precision-coverage tradeoff). K=100+ for exploration mode.
top_k.note: MRR saturates by K=50. Coverage saturates by K=100. Increasing K beyond 100 adds only noise.

quantization.finding: float16 is free lunch. int8 is model-dependent.

minilm_float16.recommendation: USE. Zero quality loss, 50% storage savings.

minilm_int8.recommendation: USABLE for MiniLM (< 5% threshold). 40% top-20 change is tolerable given overall quality.

specter2_int8.recommendation: DO NOT USE. Score compression (0.009 spread) means int8 quantization errors (0.006 max error) are 67% of the signal. Ranking becomes pure noise.

feature_input.finding: Abstract is load-bearing. Title-only collapses quality.
feature_input.recommendation: Always use title+abstract. Title-only is not a viable cost-saving measure.

cold_start.finding: MiniLM works from 1 seed. TF-IDF needs 5+.

seeds_1.interpretation: Functional but sparse. 37% coverage is above the useful threshold.

seeds_3.interpretation: Good. Coverage jumps 55% from 1 to 3 seeds.

seeds_5.interpretation: Near-saturated. This is the recommended minimum.

seeds_1.interpretation: Poor. Single seed provides too little vocabulary signal.

seeds_5.interpretation: Functional but still below MiniLM's 1-seed performance.

seeds_10.interpretation: Continuing improvement. TF-IDF does not saturate as quickly.

seeds_15.interpretation: Coverage drops -- possible overfitting to seed vocabulary.

breadth.finding: MiniLM improves with breadth. TF-IDF degrades.

s1a.interpretation: MiniLM benefits from broad interests. Broader seed sets give better centroid estimation.

s1d.interpretation: TF-IDF degrades with breadth. Broad interests dilute the keyword signal.

breadth.key_insight: The optimal strategy depends on interest vocabulary overlap with adjacent topics, not on intrinsic strategy quality.

scale.note: Scale tests used synthetic corpus duplication, which preserves vocabulary but not diversity. Real scale effects may differ.
scale.interpretation: TF-IDF's vocabulary discrimination weakens as corpus size grows. Semantic embeddings are scale-insensitive because they operate in a fixed-dimension space.

installer_recommendations.architecture: Parallel views -- each strategy as a labeled perspective, not fused ranking
installer_recommendations.architecture_rationale: Fusion degrades quality (best combo MRR 0.310 vs S1a alone 0.398). Strategies ARE complementary (Jaccard 0.179, 9 unique recoveries) but their quality dimensions are incommensurable. Present each as a named view: 'Similar papers', 'Keyword matches', 'Related communities'.

primary_view.label: Similar papers (semantic)
primary_view.why: Highest MRR (0.398), highest coverage (0.686), context-robust (works from 1 seed, improves with breadth, scale-insensitive)

secondary_views.label: Keyword matches
secondary_views.why: Best held-out recovery (5/15), widest score spread (0.072), competitive on medium-breadth topics. Provides papers MiniLM misses.
secondary_views.caveat: Degrades at scale (>10K papers) and with broad interests. Best for small-to-medium corpora.

secondary_views.label: Related communities
secondary_views.why: Unique cross-community discovery signal. Surfaces field infrastructure and emerging research directions that neither MiniLM nor TF-IDF find.
secondary_views.caveat: Cannot rank standalone (score compression). Must be presented as an unranked set or ranked by a secondary signal.

cold_start_1_seed.why: Only strategy functional at 1 seed (coverage 0.366). TF-IDF coverage at 1 seed is 0.165.

cold_start_5_seeds.why: TF-IDF becomes viable (coverage 0.253) and adds unique held-out recoveries.

narrow_interest.why: MiniLM excels on distinctive vocabulary (MRR 0.327). TF-IDF adds keyword precision for adjacent-topic discrimination.

broad_interest.why: MiniLM improves with breadth (+52% MRR). TF-IDF degrades (-27%). At broad interests, TF-IDF adds diminishing value.

small_corpus_under_5k.why: TF-IDF is more competitive at small scale (less vocabulary dilution). Both strategies viable.

large_corpus_over_10k.why: TF-IDF collapses at scale (-54% MRR at 50K). MiniLM is robust (-7.5%).

discovery_mode.why: Maximum coverage across quality dimensions. User browsing for serendipitous finds benefits from all perspectives.

defaults.negative_signals: Do NOT implement in v1. No evidence of benefit; risk of degrading small profiles.

miniLM_embeddings.note: float16 storage, one-time for 19K papers. GPU recommended, CPU viable (11 minutes).

specter2_embeddings.note: Optional, for cross-community discovery view. GPU strongly recommended (CPU would take hours).

tfidf_matrix.note: Rebuilt on startup from corpus text, not persisted.
